input.description: Explore project structure and registry system
input.prompt:
Explore the overall structure of this shadcn/ui component library project. I need to understand:

1. The top-level directory layout (what folders exist: registry, components, demos, etc.)
2. How the registry system works - find and read all registry index/definition files (e.g., registry.ts, registry/index.ts, registry/ui.ts, etc.)
3. How components are registered - look at files like registry/registry-ui.ts, registry/registry-examples.ts, or similar
4. The package.json to understand build scripts
5. Any configuration files like components.json or tailwind.config

Please read all registry-related files completely and report their full contents. I need to understand the exact schema/format for registering new components and examples.

input.description: Explore existing compound components for patterns
input.prompt:
In this shadcn/ui component library project, I need to understand how existing compound components are implemented. Please:

1. Find and fully read the source code of compound components that use React Context to share state between sub-components. Good candidates to look for:
   - Accordion (accordion.tsx)
   - Tabs (tabs.tsx)
   - Breadcrumb (breadcrumb.tsx)
   - Collapsible (collapsible.tsx)
   - Any component that exports multiple sub-components

2. For each compound component found, read the COMPLETE file contents

3. Also look at the component file structure - where are component files stored? (e.g., registry/ui/, components/ui/, etc.)

4. Look for any TypeScript/variant patterns used (e.g., cva, class-variance-authority, cn utility)

Report full file paths and complete file contents for the most relevant 2-3 compound components.

6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.

84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",

100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [

116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({

129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),

131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([

174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),

177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.

218	export const iconsSchema = z.record(

222

37	export function resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
38	  items: string[],
39	  config: Config
40	) {
41	  const registryHeaders: Record<string, Record<string, string>> = {}
42	  const resolvedItems = [...items]
43	
44	  if (!config?.registries) {
45	    setRegistryHeaders({})
46	    return resolvedItems
47	  }
48	
49	  for (let i = 0; i < resolvedItems.length; i++) {
50	    const resolved = buildUrlAndHeadersForRegistryItem(resolvedItems[i], config)
51	
52	    if (resolved) {
53	      resolvedItems[i] = resolved.url
54	
55	      if (Object.keys(resolved.headers).length > 0) {
56	        registryHeaders[resolved.url] = resolved.headers
57	      }
58	    }
59	  }
60	
61	  setRegistryHeaders(registryHeaders)
62	
63	  return resolvedItems
64	}
65	
66	// Internal function that fetches registry items without clearing context.
67	// This is used for recursive dependency resolution.
68	export async function fetchRegistryItems(
69	  items: string[],
70	  config: Config,
71	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
72	) {
73	  const results = await Promise.all(
74	    items.map(async (item) => {
75	      if (isLocalFile(item)) {
76	        return fetchRegistryLocal(item)
77	      }
78	
79	      if (isUrl(item)) {
80	        const [result] = await fetchRegistry([item], options)
81	        try {
82	          return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
83	        } catch (error) {
84	          throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
85	        }
86	      }
87	
88	      if (item.startsWith("@") && config?.registries) {
89	        const paths = resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries([item], config)
90	        const [result] = await fetchRegistry(paths, options)
91	        try {
92	          return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
93	        } catch (error) {
94	          throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
95	        }
96	      }
97	
98	      const path = `styles/${config?.style ?? "new-york-v4"}/${item}.json`
99	      const [result] = await fetchRegistry([path], options)
100	      try {
101	        return registryItemSchema.parse(result)
102	      } catch (error) {
103	        throw new RegistryParseError(item, error)
104	      }
105	    })
106	  )
107	
108	  return results
109	}

111	// Helper schema for items with source tracking.
112	const registryItemWithSourceSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
113	  .extend({
114	    type: registryItemTypeSchema,
115	    _source: z.string().optional(),
116	    // Optional fields for specific item types.
117	    font: registryItemFontSchema.optional(),
118	    config: z.any().optional(),
119	  })
120	  .passthrough()
121	
122	// Resolves a list of registry items with all their dependencies and returns
123	// a complete installation bundle with merged configuration.
124	export async function resolveRegistryTree(
125	  names: z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>["name"][],
126	  config: Config,
127	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
128	) {
129	  options = {
130	    useCache: true,
131	    ...options,
132	  }
133	
134	  let payload: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
135	  let allDependencyItems: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
136	  let allDependencyRegistryNames: string[] = []
137	
138	  const uniqueNames = Array.from(new Set(names))
139	
140	  const results = await fetchRegistryItems(uniqueNames, config, options)
141	
142	  const resultMap = new Map<string, z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>>()
143	  for (let i = 0; i < results.length; i++) {
144	    if (results[i]) {
145	      resultMap.set(uniqueNames[i], results[i])
146	    }
147	  }
148	
149	  for (const [sourceName, item] of Array.from(resultMap.entries())) {
150	    // Add source tracking
151	    const itemWithSource: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema> = {
152	      ...item,
153	      _source: sourceName,
154	    }
155	    payload.push(itemWithSource)
156	
157	    if (item.registryDependencies) {
158	      // Resolve namespace syntax and set headers for dependencies
159	      let resolvedDependencies = item.registryDependencies
160	
161	      // Check for namespaced dependencies when no registries are configured
162	      if (!config?.registries) {
163	        const namespacedDeps = item.registryDependencies.filter((dep: string) =>
164	          dep.startsWith("@")
165	        )
166	        if (namespacedDeps.length > 0) {
167	          const { registry } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(namespacedDeps[0])
168	          throw new RegistryNotConfiguredError(registry)
169	        }
170	      } else {
171	        resolvedDependencies = resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
172	          item.registryDependencies,
173	          config
174	        )
175	      }
176	
177	      const { items, registryNames } = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
178	        resolvedDependencies,
179	        config,
180	        options,
181	        new Set(uniqueNames)
182	      )
183	      allDependencyItems.push(...items)
184	      allDependencyRegistryNames.push(...registryNames)
185	    }
186	  }
187	
188	  payload.push(...allDependencyItems)
189	
190	  // Handle any remaining registry names that need index resolution
191	  if (allDependencyRegistryNames.length > 0) {
192	    // Remove duplicates from registry names
193	    const uniqueRegistryNames = Array.from(new Set(allDependencyRegistryNames))
194	
195	    // Separate namespaced and non-namespaced items
196	    const nonNamespacedItems = uniqueRegistryNames.filter(
197	      (name) => !name.startsWith("@")
198	    )
199	    const namespacedDepItems = uniqueRegistryNames.filter((name) =>
200	      name.startsWith("@")
201	    )
202	
203	    // Handle namespaced dependency items
204	    if (namespacedDepItems.length > 0) {
205	      // This will now throw specific errors on failure
206	      const depResults = await fetchRegistryItems(
207	        namespacedDepItems,
208	        config,
209	        options
210	      )
211	
212	      for (let i = 0; i < depResults.length; i++) {
213	        const item = depResults[i]
214	        const itemWithSource: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema> = {
215	          ...item,
216	          _source: namespacedDepItems[i],
217	        }
218	        payload.push(itemWithSource)
219	      }
220	    }
221	
222	    // For non-namespaced items, we need the index and style resolution
223	    if (nonNamespacedItems.length > 0) {
224	      const index = await getShadcnRegistryIndex()
225	      if (!index && payload.length === 0) {
226	        return null
227	      }
228	
229	      if (index) {
230	        // If we're resolving the index, we want it to go first
231	        if (nonNamespacedItems.includes("index")) {
232	          nonNamespacedItems.unshift("index")
233	        }
234	
235	        // Resolve non-namespaced items through the existing flow
236	        // Get URLs for all registry items including their dependencies
237	        const registryUrls: string[] = []
238	        for (const name of nonNamespacedItems) {
239	          const itemDependencies = await resolveRegistryDependencies(
240	            name,
241	            config,
242	            options
243	          )
244	          registryUrls.push(...itemDependencies)
245	        }
246	
247	        // Deduplicate URLs
248	        const uniqueUrls = Array.from(new Set(registryUrls))
249	        let result = await fetchRegistry(uniqueUrls, options)
250	        const registryPayload = z.array(registryItemSchema).parse(result)
251	        payload.push(...registryPayload)
252	      }
253	    }
254	  }
255	
256	  if (!payload.length) {
257	    return null
258	  }
259	
260	  // No deduplication - we want to support multiple items with the same name from different sources
261	
262	  // If we're resolving the index, we want to fetch
263	  // the theme item if a base color is provided.
264	  // We do this for index only.
265	  // Other components will ship with their theme tokens.
266	  if (
267	    uniqueNames.includes("index") ||
268	    allDependencyRegistryNames.includes("index")
269	  ) {
270	    if (config.tailwind.baseColor) {
271	      const theme = await registryGetTheme(config.tailwind.baseColor, config)
272	      if (theme) {
273	        payload.unshift(theme)
274	      }
275	    }
276	  }
277	
278	  // Build source map for topological sort.
279	  const sourceMap = new Map<
280	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>,
281	    string
282	  >()
283	  payload.forEach((item) => {
284	    // Use the _source property if it was added, otherwise use the name.
285	    const source = item._source || item.name
286	    sourceMap.set(item, source)
287	  })
288	
289	  // Apply topological sort to ensure dependencies come before dependents.
290	  payload = topologicalSortRegistryItems(payload, sourceMap)
291	
292	  // Sort the payload so that registry:theme items come first,
293	  // while maintaining the relative order of all items.
294	  payload.sort((a, b) => {
295	    if (a.type === "registry:theme" && b.type !== "registry:theme") {
296	      return -1
297	    }
298	    if (a.type !== "registry:theme" && b.type === "registry:theme") {
299	      return 1
300	    }
301	    return 0
302	  })
303	
304	  let tailwind = {}
305	  payload.forEach((item) => {
306	    tailwind = deepmerge(tailwind, item.tailwind ?? {})
307	  })
308	
309	  let cssVars = {}
310	  payload.forEach((item) => {
311	    cssVars = deepmerge(cssVars, item.cssVars ?? {})
312	  })
313	
314	  let css = {}
315	  payload.forEach((item) => {
316	    css = deepmerge(css, item.css ?? {})
317	  })
318	
319	  let docs = ""
320	  payload.forEach((item) => {
321	    if (item.docs) {
322	      docs += `${item.docs}\n`
323	    }
324	  })
325	
326	  let envVars = {}
327	  payload.forEach((item) => {
328	    envVars = deepmerge(envVars, item.envVars ?? {})
329	  })
330	
331	  // Deduplicate files based on resolved target paths.
332	  const deduplicatedFiles = await deduplicateFilesByTarget(
333	    payload.map((item) => item.files ?? []),
334	    config
335	  )
336	
337	  // Collect font items.
338	  const fonts: RegistryFontItem[] = payload
339	    .filter((item) => item.type === "registry:font" && item.font)
340	    .map((item) => ({
341	      ...item,
342	      type: "registry:font" as const,
343	      font: item.font!,
344	    }))
345	
346	  const parsed = registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema.parse({
347	    dependencies: deepmerge.all(payload.map((item) => item.dependencies ?? [])),
348	    devDependencies: deepmerge.all(
349	      payload.map((item) => item.devDependencies ?? [])
350	    ),
351	    files: deduplicatedFiles,
352	    tailwind,
353	    cssVars,
354	    css,
355	    docs,
356	    fonts: fonts.length > 0 ? fonts : undefined,
357	  })
358	
359	  if (Object.keys(envVars).length > 0) {
360	    parsed.envVars = envVars
361	  }
362	
363	  return parsed
364	}

366	async function resolveDependenciesRecursively(
367	  dependencies: string[],
368	  config: Config,
369	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {},
370	  visited: Set<string> = new Set()
371	) {
372	  const items: z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>[] = []
373	  const registryNames: string[] = []
374	
375	  for (const dep of dependencies) {
376	    if (visited.has(dep)) {
377	      continue
378	    }
379	    visited.add(dep)
380	
381	    // Handle URLs and local files directly.
382	    if (isUrl(dep) || isLocalFile(dep)) {
383	      const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
384	      if (item) {
385	        items.push(item)
386	        if (item.registryDependencies) {
387	          // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
388	          const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
389	            ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
390	                item.registryDependencies,
391	                config
392	              )
393	            : item.registryDependencies
394	
395	          const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
396	            resolvedDeps,
397	            config,
398	            options,
399	            visited
400	          )
401	          items.push(...nested.items)
402	          registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
403	        }
404	      }
405	    }
406	    // Handle namespaced items (e.g., @one/foo, @two/bar).
407	    else if (dep.startsWith("@") && config?.registries) {
408	      // Check if the registry exists.
409	      const { registry } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(dep)
410	      if (registry && !(registry in config.registries)) {
411	        throw new RegistryNotConfiguredError(registry)
412	      }
413	
414	      // Let getRegistryItem handle the namespaced item with config
415	      // This ensures proper authentication headers are used
416	      const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
417	      if (item) {
418	        items.push(item)
419	        if (item.registryDependencies) {
420	          // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
421	          const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
422	            ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
423	                item.registryDependencies,
424	                config
425	              )
426	            : item.registryDependencies
427	
428	          const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
429	            resolvedDeps,
430	            config,
431	            options,
432	            visited
433	          )
434	          items.push(...nested.items)
435	          registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
436	        }
437	      }
438	    }
439	    // Handle regular component names.
440	    else {
441	      registryNames.push(dep)
442	
443	      if (config) {
444	        try {
445	          const [item] = await fetchRegistryItems([dep], config, options)
446	          if (item && item.registryDependencies) {
447	            // Resolve namespaced dependencies to set proper headers.
448	            const resolvedDeps = config?.registries
449	              ? resolveRegistryItemsFromRegistries(
450	                  item.registryDependencies,
451	                  config
452	                )
453	              : item.registryDependencies
454	
455	            const nested = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
456	              resolvedDeps,
457	              config,
458	              options,
459	              visited
460	            )
461	            items.push(...nested.items)
462	            registryNames.push(...nested.registryNames)
463	          }
464	        } catch (error) {
465	          // If we can't fetch the registry item, that's okay - we'll still
466	          // include the name.
467	        }
468	      }
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  return { items, registryNames }
473	}

475	async function resolveRegistryDependencies(
476	  url: string,
477	  config: Config,
478	  options: { useCache?: boolean } = {}
479	) {
480	  if (isUrl(url)) {
481	    return [url]
482	  }
483	
484	  const { registryNames } = await resolveDependenciesRecursively(
485	    [url],
486	    config,
487	    options,
488	    new Set()
489	  )
490	
491	  const style = config.resolvedPaths?.cwd
492	    ? await getTargetStyleFromConfig(config.resolvedPaths.cwd, config.style)
493	    : config.style
494	
495	  const urls = registryNames.map((name) =>
496	    resolveRegistryUrl(isUrl(name) ? name : `styles/${style}/${name}.json`)
497	  )
498	
499	  return Array.from(new Set(urls))
500	}

578	function computeItemHash(
579	  item: Pick<z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>, "name">,
580	  source?: string
581	) {
582	  const identifier = source || item.name
583	
584	  const hash = createHash("sha256")
585	    .update(identifier)
586	    .digest("hex")
587	    .substring(0, 8)
588	
589	  return `${item.name}::${hash}`
590	}
591	
592	function extractItemIdentifierFromDependency(dependency: string) {
593	  if (isUrl(dependency)) {
594	    const url = new URL(dependency)
595	    const pathname = url.pathname
596	    const match = pathname.match(/\/([^/]+)\.json$/)
597	    const name = match ? match[1] : path.basename(pathname, ".json")
598	
599	    return {
600	      name,
601	      hash: computeItemHash({ name }, dependency),
602	    }
603	  }
604	
605	  if (isLocalFile(dependency)) {
606	    const match = dependency.match(/\/([^/]+)\.json$/)
607	    const name = match ? match[1] : path.basename(dependency, ".json")
608	
609	    return {
610	      name,
611	      hash: computeItemHash({ name }, dependency),
612	    }
613	  }
614	
615	  const { item } = parseRegistryAndItemFromString(dependency)
616	  return {
617	    name: item,
618	    hash: computeItemHash({ name: item }, dependency),
619	  }
620	}
621	
622	function topologicalSortRegistryItems(
623	  items: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[],
624	  sourceMap: Map<z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>, string>
625	) {
626	  const itemMap = new Map<
627	    string,
628	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>
629	  >()
630	  const hashToItem = new Map<
631	    string,
632	    z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>
633	  >()
634	  const inDegree = new Map<string, number>()
635	  const adjacencyList = new Map<string, string[]>()
636	
637	  items.forEach((item) => {
638	    const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
639	    const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
640	
641	    itemMap.set(hash, item)
642	    hashToItem.set(hash, item)
643	    inDegree.set(hash, 0)
644	    adjacencyList.set(hash, [])
645	  })
646	
647	  // Build a map of dependency to possible items.
648	  const depToHashes = new Map<string, string[]>()
649	  items.forEach((item) => {
650	    const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
651	    const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
652	
653	    if (!depToHashes.has(item.name)) {
654	      depToHashes.set(item.name, [])
655	    }
656	    depToHashes.get(item.name)!.push(hash)
657	
658	    if (source !== item.name) {
659	      if (!depToHashes.has(source)) {
660	        depToHashes.set(source, [])
661	      }
662	      depToHashes.get(source)!.push(hash)
663	    }
664	  })
665	
666	  items.forEach((item) => {
667	    const itemSource = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
668	    const itemHash = computeItemHash(item, itemSource)
669	
670	    if (item.registryDependencies) {
671	      item.registryDependencies.forEach((dep) => {
672	        let depHash: string | undefined
673	
674	        const exactMatches = depToHashes.get(dep) || []
675	        if (exactMatches.length === 1) {
676	          depHash = exactMatches[0]
677	        } else if (exactMatches.length > 1) {
678	          // Multiple matches - try to disambiguate.
679	          // For now, just use the first one and warn.
680	          depHash = exactMatches[0]
681	        } else {
682	          const { name } = extractItemIdentifierFromDependency(dep)
683	          const nameMatches = depToHashes.get(name) || []
684	          if (nameMatches.length > 0) {
685	            depHash = nameMatches[0]
686	          }
687	        }
688	
689	        if (depHash && itemMap.has(depHash)) {
690	          adjacencyList.get(depHash)!.push(itemHash)
691	          inDegree.set(itemHash, inDegree.get(itemHash)! + 1)
692	        }
693	      })
694	    }
695	  })
696	
697	  // Implements Kahn's algorithm.
698	  const queue: string[] = []
699	  const sorted: z.infer<typeof registryItemWithSourceSchema>[] = []
700	
701	  inDegree.forEach((degree, hash) => {
702	    if (degree === 0) {
703	      queue.push(hash)
704	    }
705	  })
706	
707	  while (queue.length > 0) {
708	    const currentHash = queue.shift()!
709	    const item = itemMap.get(currentHash)!
710	    sorted.push(item)
711	
712	    adjacencyList.get(currentHash)!.forEach((dependentHash) => {
713	      const newDegree = inDegree.get(dependentHash)! - 1
714	      inDegree.set(dependentHash, newDegree)
715	
716	      if (newDegree === 0) {
717	        queue.push(dependentHash)
718	      }
719	    })
720	  }
721	
722	  if (sorted.length !== items.length) {
723	    // console.warn("Circular dependency detected in registry items")
724	    // Return all items even if there are circular dependencies
725	    // Items not in sorted are part of circular dependencies
726	    const sortedHashes = new Set(
727	      sorted.map((item) => {
728	        const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
729	        return computeItemHash(item, source)
730	      })
731	    )
732	
733	    items.forEach((item) => {
734	      const source = sourceMap.get(item) || item.name
735	      const hash = computeItemHash(item, source)
736	      if (!sortedHashes.has(hash)) {
737	        sorted.push(item)
738	      }
739	    })
740	  }
741	
742	  return sorted
743	}

1	import { searchResultItemSchema, searchResultsSchema } from "@/src/schema"
2	import { Config } from "@/src/utils/get-config"
3	import fuzzysort from "fuzzysort"
4	import { z } from "zod"
5	
6	import { getRegistry } from "./api"
7	
8	export async function searchRegistries(
9	  registries: string[],
10	  options?: {
11	    query?: string
12	    limit?: number
13	    offset?: number
14	    config?: Partial<Config>
15	    useCache?: boolean
16	  }
17	) {
18	  const { query, limit, offset, config, useCache } = options || {}
19	
20	  let allItems: z.infer<typeof searchResultItemSchema>[] = []
21	
22	  for (const registry of registries) {
23	    const registryData = await getRegistry(registry, { config, useCache })
24	
25	    const itemsWithRegistry = (registryData.items || []).map((item) => ({
26	      name: item.name,
27	      type: item.type,
28	      description: item.description,
29	      registry: registry,
30	      addCommandArgument: buildRegistryItemNameFromRegistry(
31	        item.name,
32	        registry
33	      ),
34	    }))
35	
36	    allItems = allItems.concat(itemsWithRegistry)
37	  }
38	
39	  if (query) {
40	    allItems = searchItems(allItems, {
41	      query,
42	      limit: allItems.length,
43	      keys: ["name", "description"],
44	    }) as z.infer<typeof searchResultItemSchema>[]
45	  }
46	
47	  const paginationOffset = offset || 0
48	  const paginationLimit = limit || allItems.length
49	  const totalItems = allItems.length
50	
51	  const result: z.infer<typeof searchResultsSchema> = {
52	    pagination: {
53	      total: totalItems,
54	      offset: paginationOffset,
55	      limit: paginationLimit,
56	      hasMore: paginationOffset + paginationLimit < totalItems,
57	    },
58	    items: allItems.slice(paginationOffset, paginationOffset + paginationLimit),
59	  }
60	
61	  return searchResultsSchema.parse(result)
62	}
63	
64	const searchableItemSchema = z
65	  .object({
66	    name: z.string(),
67	    type: z.string().optional(),
68	    description: z.string().optional(),
69	    registry: z.string().optional(),
70	    addCommandArgument: z.string().optional(),
71	  })
72	  .passthrough()
73	
74	type SearchableItem = z.infer<typeof searchableItemSchema>
75	
76	function searchItems<
77	  T extends {
78	    name: string
79	    type?: string
80	    description?: string
81	    addCommandArgument?: string
82	    [key: string]: any
83	  } = SearchableItem,
84	>(
85	  items: T[],
86	  options: {
87	    query: string
88	  } & Pick<Parameters<typeof fuzzysort.go>[2], "keys" | "threshold" | "limit">
89	) {
90	  options = {
91	    limit: 100,
92	    threshold: -10000,
93	    ...options,
94	  }
95	
96	  const searchResults = fuzzysort.go(options.query, items, {
97	    keys: options.keys,
98	    threshold: options.threshold,
99	    limit: options.limit,
100	  })
101	
102	  const results = searchResults.map((result) => result.obj)
103	
104	  return z.array(searchableItemSchema).parse(results)
105	}
106	
107	function isUrl(string: string): boolean {
108	  try {
109	    new URL(string)
110	    return true
111	  } catch {
112	    return false
113	  }
114	}
115	
116	// Builds the registry item name for the add command.
117	// For namespaced registries, returns "registry/item".
118	// For URL registries, replaces "registry" with the item name in the URL.
119	export function buildRegistryItemNameFromRegistry(
120	  name: string,
121	  registry: string
122	) {
123	  // If registry is not a URL, return namespace format.
124	  if (!isUrl(registry)) {
125	    return `${registry}/${name}`
126	  }
127	
128	  // Find where the host part ends in the original string.
129	  const protocolEnd = registry.indexOf("://") + 3
130	  const hostEnd = registry.indexOf("/", protocolEnd)
131	
132	  if (hostEnd === -1) {
133	    // No path, check for query params.
134	    const queryStart = registry.indexOf("?", protocolEnd)
135	    if (queryStart !== -1) {
136	      // Has query params but no path.
137	      const beforeQuery = registry.substring(0, queryStart)
138	      const queryAndAfter = registry.substring(queryStart)
139	      // Replace "registry" with itemName in query params only.
140	      const updatedQuery = queryAndAfter.replace(/\bregistry\b/g, name)
141	      return beforeQuery + updatedQuery
142	    }
143	    // No path or query, return as is.
144	    return registry
145	  }
146	
147	  // Split at host boundary.
148	  const hostPart = registry.substring(0, hostEnd)
149	  const pathAndQuery = registry.substring(hostEnd)
150	
151	  // Find all occurrences of "registry" in path and query.
152	  // Replace only the last occurrence in the path segment.
153	  const pathEnd =
154	    pathAndQuery.indexOf("?") !== -1
155	      ? pathAndQuery.indexOf("?")
156	      : pathAndQuery.length
157	  const pathOnly = pathAndQuery.substring(0, pathEnd)
158	  const queryAndAfter = pathAndQuery.substring(pathEnd)
159	
160	  // Replace the last occurrence of "registry" in the path.
161	  const lastIndex = pathOnly.lastIndexOf("registry")
162	  let updatedPath = pathOnly
163	  if (lastIndex !== -1) {
164	    updatedPath =
165	      pathOnly.substring(0, lastIndex) +
166	      name +
167	      pathOnly.substring(lastIndex + "registry".length)
168	  }
169	
170	  // Replace all occurrences of "registry" in query params.
171	  const updatedQuery = queryAndAfter.replace(/\bregistry\b/g, name)
172	
173	  return hostPart + updatedPath + updatedQuery
174	}

1	# Examples
2	
3	This directory contains demo components for the component documentation.
4	
5	## Directory Structure
6	
7	```
8	examples
9	├── base
10	│   ├── button-demo.tsx
11	│   └── ...
12	├── radix
13	│   └── ...
14	└── __index__.tsx    # Auto-generated by registry:build
15	```
16	
17	## Adding a New Example
18	
19	1. Create a new `.tsx` file in `examples/base` or `examples/radix`:
20	
21	```tsx
22	// examples/base/button-loading.tsx
23	import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button"
24	
25	export function ButtonLoading() {
26	  return <Button disabled>Loading...</Button>
27	}
28	```
29	
30	2. Run the registry build to regenerate the index:
31	
32	```bash
33	pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build
34	```
35	
36	3. Use the example in documentation by referencing its name (filename without `.tsx`):
37	
38	```tsx
39	<ComponentPreview name="button-loading" />
40	```
41	
42	## Notes
43	
44	- Example files should be placed directly in `examples/base` or `examples/radix`, not in subdirectories.
45	- Base examples should import from `@/styles/base-nova/*`. Radix examples should import from `@/styles/radix-nova/*`.
46	- Both named exports and default exports are supported.
47	- After adding or removing examples, run `pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build` to update the index.
48

3	import { Config, createConfig, DeepPartial } from "@/src/utils/get-config"

6	function resolveStyleFromConfig(config: DeepPartial<Config>) {
7	  if (!config.style) {
8	    return FALLBACK_STYLE
9	  }
10	
11	  // Check if we should use new-york-v4 for Tailwind v4.
12	  // We assume that if tailwind.config is empty, we're using Tailwind v4.
13	  if (config.style === "new-york" && config.tailwind?.config === "") {
14	    return FALLBACK_STYLE
15	  }
16	
17	  return config.style
18	}
19	
20	export function configWithDefaults(config?: DeepPartial<Config>) {
21	  const baseConfig = createConfig({
22	    style: FALLBACK_STYLE,
23	    registries: BUILTIN_REGISTRIES,
24	  })
25	
26	  if (!config) {
27	    return baseConfig

20	const FILE_EXTENSIONS_FOR_LOOKUP = [".tsx", ".ts", ".jsx", ".js", ".css"]
21	const FILE_PATH_SKIP_LIST = ["lib/utils.ts"]
22	const DEPENDENCY_SKIP_LIST = [
23	  /^(react|react-dom|next)(\/.*)?$/, // Matches react, react-dom, next and their submodules
24	  /^(node|jsr|npm):.*$/, // Matches node:, jsr:, and npm: prefixed modules
25	]
26	
27	const project = new Project({
28	  compilerOptions: {},
29	})

31	// This returns the dependency from the module specifier.
32	// Here dependency means an npm package.
33	export function getDependencyFromModuleSpecifier(
34	  moduleSpecifier: string
35	): string | null {
36	  // Skip if the dependency matches any pattern in the skip list
37	  if (DEPENDENCY_SKIP_LIST.some((pattern) => pattern.test(moduleSpecifier))) {
38	    return null
39	  }
40	
41	  // If the module specifier does not start with `@` and has a /, add the dependency first part only.
42	  // E.g. `foo/bar` -> `foo`
43	  if (!moduleSpecifier.startsWith("@") && moduleSpecifier.includes("/")) {
44	    moduleSpecifier = moduleSpecifier.split("/")[0]
45	  }
46	
47	  // For scoped packages, we want to keep the first two parts
48	  // E.g. `@types/react/dom` -> `@types/react`
49	  if (moduleSpecifier.startsWith("@")) {
50	    const parts = moduleSpecifier.split("/")
51	    if (parts.length > 2) {
52	      moduleSpecifier = parts.slice(0, 2).join("/")
53	    }
54	  }
55	
56	  return moduleSpecifier
57	}

59	export async function recursivelyResolveFileImports(
60	  filePath: string,
61	  config: z.infer<typeof configSchema>,
62	  projectInfo: ProjectInfo,
63	  processedFiles: Set<string> = new Set()
64	): Promise<Pick<z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>, "files" | "dependencies">> {
65	  const resolvedFilePath = path.resolve(config.resolvedPaths.cwd, filePath)
66	  const relativeRegistryFilePath = path.relative(
67	    config.resolvedPaths.cwd,
68	    resolvedFilePath
69	  )
70	
71	  // Skip if the file is in the skip list
72	  if (FILE_PATH_SKIP_LIST.includes(relativeRegistryFilePath)) {
73	    return { dependencies: [], files: [] }
74	  }
75	
76	  // Skip if the file extension is not one of the supported extensions
77	  const fileExtension = path.extname(filePath)
78	  if (!FILE_EXTENSIONS_FOR_LOOKUP.includes(fileExtension)) {
79	    return { dependencies: [], files: [] }
80	  }
81	
82	  // Prevent infinite loop: skip if already processed
83	  if (processedFiles.has(relativeRegistryFilePath)) {
84	    return { dependencies: [], files: [] }
85	  }
86	  processedFiles.add(relativeRegistryFilePath)
87	
88	  const stat = await fs.stat(resolvedFilePath)
89	  if (!stat.isFile()) {
90	    // Optionally log or handle this case
91	    return { dependencies: [], files: [] }
92	  }
93	
94	  const content = await fs.readFile(resolvedFilePath, "utf-8")
95	  const tempFile = await createTempSourceFile(path.basename(resolvedFilePath))
96	  const sourceFile = project.createSourceFile(tempFile, content, {
97	    scriptKind: ScriptKind.TSX,
98	  })
99	  const tsConfig = await loadConfig(config.resolvedPaths.cwd)
100	  if (tsConfig.resultType === "failed") {
101	    return { dependencies: [], files: [] }
102	  }
103	
104	  const files: z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>["files"] = []
105	  const dependencies = new Set<string>()
106	
107	  // Add the original file first
108	  const fileType = determineFileType(filePath)
109	  const originalFile = {
110	    path: relativeRegistryFilePath,
111	    type: fileType,
112	    target: "",
113	  }
114	  files.push(originalFile)
115	
116	  // 1. Find all import statements in the file.
117	  const importStatements = sourceFile.getImportDeclarations()
118	  for (const importStatement of importStatements) {
119	    const moduleSpecifier = importStatement.getModuleSpecifierValue()
120	
121	    const isRelativeImport = moduleSpecifier.startsWith(".")
122	    const isAliasImport = moduleSpecifier.startsWith(
123	      `${projectInfo.aliasPrefix}/`
124	    )
125	
126	    // If not a local import, add to the dependencies array.
127	    if (!isAliasImport && !isRelativeImport) {
128	      const dependency = getDependencyFromModuleSpecifier(moduleSpecifier)
129	      if (dependency) {
130	        dependencies.add(dependency)
131	      }
132	      continue
133	    }
134	
135	    let probableImportFilePath = await resolveImport(moduleSpecifier, tsConfig)
136	
137	    if (isRelativeImport) {
138	      probableImportFilePath = path.resolve(
139	        path.dirname(resolvedFilePath),
140	        moduleSpecifier
141	      )
142	    }
143	
144	    if (!probableImportFilePath) {
145	      continue
146	    }
147	
148	    // Check if the probable import path has a file extension.
149	    // Try each extension until we find a file that exists.
150	    const hasExtension = path.extname(probableImportFilePath)
151	    if (!hasExtension) {
152	      for (const ext of FILE_EXTENSIONS_FOR_LOOKUP) {
153	        const pathWithExt: string = `${probableImportFilePath}${ext}`
154	        try {
155	          await fs.access(pathWithExt)
156	          probableImportFilePath = pathWithExt
157	          break
158	        } catch {
159	          continue
160	        }
161	      }
162	    }
163	
164	    const nestedRelativeRegistryFilePath = path.relative(
165	      config.resolvedPaths.cwd,
166	      probableImportFilePath
167	    )
168	
169	    // Skip if we've already processed this file or if it's in the skip list
170	    if (
171	      processedFiles.has(nestedRelativeRegistryFilePath) ||
172	      FILE_PATH_SKIP_LIST.includes(nestedRelativeRegistryFilePath)
173	    ) {
174	      continue
175	    }
176	
177	    const fileType = determineFileType(moduleSpecifier)
178	    const file = {
179	      path: nestedRelativeRegistryFilePath,
180	      type: fileType,
181	      target: "",
182	    }
183	
184	    // TODO (shadcn): fix this.
185	    if (fileType === "registry:page" || fileType === "registry:file") {
186	      file.target = moduleSpecifier
187	    }
188	
189	    files.push(file)
190	
191	    // Recursively process the imported file, passing the shared processedFiles set
192	    const nestedResults = await recursivelyResolveFileImports(
193	      nestedRelativeRegistryFilePath,
194	      config,
195	      projectInfo,
196	      processedFiles
197	    )
198	
199	    if (nestedResults.files) {
200	      // Only add files that haven't been processed yet
201	      for (const file of nestedResults.files) {
202	        if (!processedFiles.has(file.path)) {
203	          processedFiles.add(file.path)
204	          files.push(file)
205	        }
206	      }
207	    }
208	
209	    if (nestedResults.dependencies) {
210	      nestedResults.dependencies.forEach((dep) => dependencies.add(dep))
211	    }
212	  }
213	
214	  // Deduplicate files by path
215	  const uniqueFiles = Array.from(
216	    new Map(files.map((file) => [file.path, file])).values()
217	  )
218	
219	  return {
220	    dependencies: Array.from(dependencies),
221	    files: uniqueFiles,
222	  }
223	}

225	async function createTempSourceFile(filename: string) {
226	  const dir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(tmpdir(), "shadcn-"))
227	  return path.join(dir, filename)
228	}

230	// This is a bit tricky to accurately determine.
231	// For now we'll use the module specifier to determine the type.
232	function determineFileType(
233	  moduleSpecifier: string
234	): z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>["type"] {
235	  if (moduleSpecifier.includes("/ui/")) {
236	    return "registry:ui"
237	  }
238	
239	  if (moduleSpecifier.includes("/lib/")) {
240	    return "registry:lib"
241	  }
242	
243	  if (moduleSpecifier.includes("/hooks/")) {
244	    return "registry:hook"
245	  }
246	
247	  if (moduleSpecifier.includes("/components/")) {
248	    return "registry:component"
249	  }
250	
251	  return "registry:component"
252	}

254	// Additional utility functions for local file support
255	export function isUrl(path: string) {
256	  try {
257	    new URL(path)
258	    return true
259	  } catch (error) {
260	    return false
261	  }
262	}
263	
264	export function isLocalFile(path: string) {
265	  return path.endsWith(".json") && !isUrl(path)
266	}

268	/**
269	 * Check if a registry item is universal (framework-agnostic).
270	 * A universal registry item must:
271	 * 1. Have type "registry:item" or "registry:file"
272	 * 2. If it has files, all files must have explicit targets and be type "registry:file" or "registry:item"
273	 * It can be installed without framework detection or components.json.
274	 */
275	export function isUniversalRegistryItem(
276	  registryItem:
277	    | Pick<z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>, "files" | "type">
278	    | null
279	    | undefined
280	): boolean {
281	  if (!registryItem) {
282	    return false
283	  }
284	
285	  if (
286	    registryItem.type !== "registry:item" &&
287	    registryItem.type !== "registry:file"
288	  ) {
289	    return false
290	  }
291	
292	  const files = registryItem.files ?? []
293	
294	  // If there are files, all must have targets and be of type registry:file or registry:item.
295	  return files.every(
296	    (file) =>
297	      !!file.target &&
298	      (file.type === "registry:file" || file.type === "registry:item")
299	  )
300	}
301	
302	// Deduplicates files based on their resolved target paths.
303	// When multiple files resolve to the same target path, the last one wins.
304	export async function deduplicateFilesByTarget(
305	  filesArrays: Array<z.infer<typeof registryItemFileSchema>[] | undefined>,
306	  config: Config
307	) {
308	  // Fallback to simple concatenation when we don't have complete config.
309	  if (!canDeduplicateFiles(config)) {
310	    return z
311	      .array(registryItemFileSchema)
312	      .parse(filesArrays.flat().filter(Boolean))
313	  }
314	
315	  // Get project info for file resolution.
316	  const projectInfo = await getProjectInfo(config.resolvedPaths.cwd)
317	  const targetMap = new Map<string, z.infer<typeof registryItemFileSchema>>()
318	  const allFiles = z
319	    .array(registryItemFileSchema)
320	    .parse(filesArrays.flat().filter(Boolean))
321	
322	  allFiles.forEach((file) => {
323	    const resolvedPath = resolveFilePath(file, config, {
324	      isSrcDir: projectInfo?.isSrcDir,
325	      framework: projectInfo?.framework.name,
326	      commonRoot: findCommonRoot(
327	        allFiles.map((f) => f.path),
328	        file.path
329	      ),
330	    })
331	
332	    if (resolvedPath) {
333	      // Last one wins - overwrites previous entry.
334	      targetMap.set(resolvedPath, file)
335	    }
336	  })
337	
338	  return Array.from(targetMap.values())
339	}
340	
341	// Checks if the config has the minimum required paths for file deduplication.
342	export function canDeduplicateFiles(config: Config) {
343	  return !!(
344	    config?.resolvedPaths?.cwd &&
345	    (config?.resolvedPaths?.ui ||
346	      config?.resolvedPaths?.lib ||
347	      config?.resolvedPaths?.components ||
348	      config?.resolvedPaths?.hooks)
349	  )
350	}

1	# Registry bases (`base` and `radix`)
2	
3	This folder holds **two parallel registries**:
4	
5	- **`base/`** — Base UI–backed components and blocks  
6	- **`radix/`** — Radix-backed components and blocks  
7	
8	## Keep them in sync
9	
10	For any **shared** surface (same preview block, same card, same example intent), changes should be applied to **both** `base` and `radix` variants.
11	
12	- Adjust only what must differ: imports (`.../base/ui/...` vs `.../radix/ui/...`) and primitive APIs.
13	- Avoid editing only one tree unless the work is intentionally scoped to a single base.
14	
15	Project automation: see `.cursor/rules/registry-bases-parity.mdc` for the Cursor rule agents use when working under `apps/v4/registry/bases/`.
16

1	# Contributing
2	
3	Thanks for your interest in contributing to ui.shadcn.com. We're happy to have you here.
4	
5	Please take a moment to review this document before submitting your first pull request. We also strongly recommend that you check for open issues and pull requests to see if someone else is working on something similar.
6	
7	If you need any help, feel free to reach out to [@shadcn](https://twitter.com/shadcn).
8	
9	## About this repository
10	
11	This repository is a monorepo.
12	
13	- We use [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) and [`workspaces`](https://pnpm.io/workspaces) for development.
14	- We use [Turborepo](https://turbo.build/repo) as our build system.
15	- We use [changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for managing releases.
16	
17	## Structure
18	
19	This repository is structured as follows:
20	
21	```
22	apps
23	└── v4
24	    ├── app
25	    ├── components
26	    ├── content
27	    └── registry
28	        └── new-york-v4
29	            ├── example
30	            └── ui
31	packages
32	└── shadcn
33	```
34	
35	| Path                 | Description                              |
36	| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
37	| `apps/v4/app`        | The Next.js application for the website. |
38	| `apps/v4/components` | The React components for the website.    |
39	| `apps/v4/content`    | The content for the website.             |
40	| `apps/v4/registry`   | The registry for the components.         |
41	| `packages/shadcn`    | The `shadcn` package.                    |
42	
43	## Development
44	
45	### Fork this repo
46	
47	You can fork this repo by clicking the fork button in the top right corner of this page.
48	
49	### Clone on your local machine
50	
51	```bash
52	git clone https://github.com/your-username/ui.git
53	```
54	
55	### Navigate to project directory
56	
57	```bash
58	cd ui
59	```
60	
61	### Create a new Branch
62	
63	```bash
64	git checkout -b my-new-branch
65	```
66	
67	### Install dependencies
68	
69	```bash
70	pnpm install
71	```
72	
73	### Run a workspace
74	
75	You can use the `pnpm --filter=[WORKSPACE]` command to start the development process for a workspace.
76	
77	#### Examples
78	
79	1. To run the `ui.shadcn.com` website:
80	
81	```bash
82	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
83	```
84	
85	2. To run the `shadcn` package:
86	
87	```bash
88	pnpm --filter=shadcn dev
89	```
90	
91	## Running the CLI Locally
92	
93	To run the CLI locally, you can follow the workflow:
94	
95	1. Start by running the dev server:
96	
97	   ```bash
98	   pnpm dev
99	   ```
100	
101	2. In another terminal tab, test the CLI by running:
102	
103	   ```bash
104	   pnpm shadcn
105	   ```
106	
107	   To test the CLI in a specific app, use a command like:
108	
109	   ```bash
110	   pnpm shadcn <init | add | ...> -c ~/Desktop/my-app
111	   ```
112	
113	This workflow ensures that you are running the most recent version of the registry and testing the CLI properly in your local environment.
114	
115	## Documentation
116	
117	The documentation for this project is located in the `v4` workspace. You can run the documentation locally by running the following command:
118	
119	```bash
120	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
121	```
122	
123	Documentation is written using [MDX](https://mdxjs.com). You can find the documentation files in the `apps/v4/content/docs` directory.
124	
125	## Components
126	
127	We use a registry system for developing components. You can find the source code for the components under `apps/v4/registry`. The components are organized by styles.
128	
129	```bash
130	apps
131	└── v4
132	    └── registry
133	        └── new-york-v4
134	            ├── example
135	            └── ui
136	```
137	
138	When adding or modifying components, please ensure that:
139	
140	1. You make the changes for every style.
141	2. You update the documentation.
142	3. You run `pnpm registry:build` to update the registry.
143	
144	## Commit Convention
145	
146	Before you create a Pull Request, please check whether your commits comply with
147	the commit conventions used in this repository.
148	
149	When you create a commit we kindly ask you to follow the convention
150	`category(scope or module): message` in your commit message while using one of

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report. Let me compile all the information:

## Comprehensive Structure Report: shadcn/ui Component Library Project

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY LAYOUT

```
/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493747639-5dcie3/
├── apps/                      # Application demos and examples
│   └── v4/                     # The main website/demo app
├── packages/                   # Core packages
│   ├── shadcn/                 # Main shadcn CLI and registry system
│   └── tests/                  # Test suite
├── templates/                  # Starter templates for different frameworks
│   ├── next-app/
│   ├── next-monorepo/
│   ├── vite-app/
│   ├── astro-app/
│   ├── react-router-app/
│   └── ... (more templates)
├── scripts/                    # Build/utility scripts
├── skills/                     # Agent skills
├── .changeset/                 # Changesets for version management
├── .cursor/                    # Cursor IDE config
└── .github/                    # GitHub Actions workflows and templates
```

### 2. REGISTRY SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

The registry system is **decoupled and highly modular**. Here are the core concepts:

#### Registry File Structure (`/packages/shadcn/src/registry/`)

```
registry/
├── index.ts                 # Main exports
├── schema.ts                # Zod schemas for all registry types
├── api.ts                   # High-level API for fetching registries & items
├── resolver.ts              # Resolves dependencies & builds item trees
├── builder.ts               # Builds URLs & headers from registry configs
├── fetcher.ts               # Low-level HTTP fetching
├── parser.ts                # Parses registry@item syntax
├── namespaces.ts            # Discovers registry namespaces
├── search.ts                # Fuzzy search across registries
├── constants.ts             # Builtin registries, URLs, colors
├── config.ts                # Config-based utilities (deps, files)
├── utils.ts                 # Utility functions (URL check, dedup, etc.)
├── context.ts               # Registry context management
├── errors.ts                # Registry-specific errors
├── env.ts                   # Environment variable expansion
└── validator.ts             # Config validation
```

### 3. REGISTRY ITEM SCHEMA - COMPLETE SPEC

From `schema.ts`, here are the core types that define how registry items work:

#### Registry Item Types
```typescript
// From registryItemTypeSchema enum:
"registry:lib"        // Library utilities
"registry:block"      // UI blocks/layouts
"registry:component"  // React components
"registry:ui"         // UI primitives (buttons, inputs, etc)
"registry:hook"       // Custom React hooks
"registry:page"       // Full page components
"registry:file"       // Generic files
"registry:theme"      // Theme configurations
"registry:style"      // CSS styles
"registry:item"       // Generic item (universal format)
"registry:base"       // Base configuration
"registry:font"       // Font definitions
"registry:example"    // Internal examples
"registry:internal"   // Internal use only
```

#### Common Item Schema
Every registry item has these base fields:
```typescript
{
  $schema?: string                    // Schema version reference
  extends?: string                    // Inherit from another item
  name: string                        // Unique identifier
  title?: string                      // Display name
  author?: string                     // Minimum 2 chars
  description?: string                // Brief description
  dependencies?: string[]             // NPM packages (e.g., "react@18")
  devDependencies?: string[]          // Dev packages
  registryDependencies?: string[]     // Other registry items (e.g., "@shadcn/button")
  files?: RegistryItemFile[]          // Files to install
  tailwind?: TailwindConfig           // Tailwind configuration
  cssVars?: CSSVariables              // CSS variable definitions
  css?: CSSProperties                 # CSS snippets to inject
  envVars?: Record<string, string>    // Environment variables
  meta?: Record<string, any>          // Arbitrary metadata
  docs?: string                       // Documentation/markdown
  categories?: string[]               # Categories for organization
  type: ItemType                      # Discriminator field (required)
}
```

#### File Definition Schema
Files are discriminated by type:
```typescript
// For registry:file and registry:page (requires target):
{
  path: string              // Relative path to file
  content?: string          // File content (optional, fetched if missing)
  type: "registry:file" | "registry:page"
  target: string            // Required: where to place file
}

// For all other types (target optional):
{
  path: string              // Relative path
  content?: string          // File content
  type: RegistryItemType    // Component, UI, lib, hook, etc.
  target?: string           // Optional destination override
}
```

#### Tailwind Configuration
```typescript
{
  config?: {
    content?: string[]      // Tailwind content patterns
    theme?: Record<string, any>  // Theme extensions
    plugins?: string[]      // Plugin names
  }
}
```

#### CSS Variables Schema
```typescript
{
  theme?: Record<string, string>   // Default vars
  light?: Record<string, string>   // Light mode overrides
  dark?: Record<string, string>    // Dark mode overrides
}
```

#### Font Item Schema (special case)
```typescript
{
  type: "registry:font"
  font: {
    family: string              // Font name
    provider: "google"          // Only Google supported currently
    import: string              # Import statement
    variable: string            # CSS variable name
    weight?: string[]           # Font weights
    subsets?: string[]          # Font subsets
    selector?: string           # CSS selector override
    dependency?: string         # NPM package dependency
  }
}
```

#### Base Item Schema (special case)
```typescript
{
  type: "registry:base"
  config?: DeepPartial<RawConfig>  // Partial components.json config
}
```

### 4. HOW COMPONENTS ARE REGISTERED

#### Registry URL Structure
The built-in shadcn registry uses this URL pattern:
```typescript
"@shadcn" => "${REGISTRY_URL}/styles/{style}/{name}.json"
```

Example URLs:
- `https://ui.shadcn.com/r/styles/new-york-v4/button.json`
- `https://ui.shadcn.com/r/index.json` (main index)
- `https://ui.shadcn.com/r/styles/index.json`
- `https://ui.shadcn.com/r/colors/zinc.json`

#### Custom Registry Configuration
In `components.json`, you can register custom registries:
```json
{
  "registries": {
    "@acme": "https://registry.acme.com/{name}.json",
    "@custom": {
      "url": "https://api.example.com/registry/{name}",
      "params": {
        "apiKey": "${CUSTOM_REGISTRY_API_KEY}"
      },
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer ${AUTH_TOKEN}"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

#### Registry Item Naming
Items can be referenced as:
- **Built-in**: `button`, `input`, `dialog`
- **Namespaced**: `@acme/button`, `@custom/my-component`
- **URLs**: Full HTTP(S) URLs
- **Local files**: JSON files with `.json` extension

#### Dependency Resolution
The resolver (`resolver.ts`) handles:
1. **Direct dependencies**: Listed in `registryDependencies`
2. **Recursive resolution**: Fetches dependencies of dependencies
3. **Deduplication**: Ensures no duplicate items using topological sort
4. **File merging**: Combines files from all items in dependency tree
5. **Configuration merging**: Deep merges tailwind, cssVars, css configs

### 5. PACKAGE.JSON - BUILD & SCRIPTS

Key build information from `/packages/shadcn/package.json`:

**Build Tools:**
- `tsup` - TypeScript bundler
- `vitest` - Test runner
- `typescript` - TS compiler
- `ts-morph` - AST manipulation

**Key Dependencies:**
- `@babel/core`, `@babel/parser` - Code parsing
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` - MCP support
- `commander` - CLI framework
- `cosmiconfig` - Config file loader
- `deepmerge` - Deep object merging
- `fast-glob` - File globbing
- `zod` - Schema validation
- `tailwind-merge` - Tailwind utility merging
- `fuzzysort` - Fuzzy search
- `recast` - Code printing/formatting

**Build Scripts:**
```bash
npm run build              # Build with tsup
npm run dev               # Watch mode
npm run typecheck         # Type checking
npm start                 # Run CLI (uses dist/index.js)
npm test                  # Run vitest
npm run format:write      # Prettier formatting
npm run mcp:inspect       # Inspect MCP implementation
```

**Environment Variables for Local Development:**
```bash
REGISTRY_URL              # Registry base URL (default: https://ui.shadcn.com/r)
SHADCN_TEMPLATE_DIR       # Template directory path (for development)
```

### 6. CONFIGURATION FILES

#### components.json Schema
Located in `apps/v4/components.json` (example):
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json",
  "style": "new-york" | "new-york-v4",
  "rsc": true,                            // React Server Components
  "tsx": true,                            // Use TSX files
  "tailwind": {
    "config": "tailwind.config.ts",       // Path to tailwind config
    "css": "app/globals.css",             // Main CSS file
    "baseColor": "neutral",               // Theme color
    "cssVariables": true,                 // Use CSS variables
    "prefix": ""                          // Tailwind prefix
  },
  "aliases": {
    "components": "@/components",         // Component import alias
    "utils": "@/lib/utils",               // Utils alias
    "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui",    // UI components alias
    "lib": "@/lib",                       // Lib alias
    "hooks": "@/hooks"                    // Hooks alias
  },
  "iconLibrary": "lucide",                // Icon library
  "rtl": false,                           // Right-to-left support (optional)
  "menuColor": "default",                 // Menu styling
  "menuAccent": "subtle"                  // Menu accent style
}
```

#### Resolved Config Schema
After loading from components.json, config includes:
```typescript
{
  resolvedPaths: {
    cwd: string                           // Current working directory
    tailwindConfig: string                // Resolved tailwind config path
    tailwindCss: string                   # Resolved CSS file path
    utils: string                         // Resolved utils directory
    components: string                    // Resolved components directory
    lib: string                           // Resolved lib directory
    hooks: string                         // Resolved hooks directory
    ui: string                            // Resolved ui directory
  }
}
```

### 7. REGISTRY API EXPORTS

From `registry/index.ts`:
```typescript
// Main functions
export async function getRegistry(name: string, options?)
export async function getRegistryItems(items: string[], options?)
export async function resolveRegistryItems(items: string[], options?)
export async function getRegistries(options?)
export async function getRegistriesIndex(options?)  // Deprecated
export async function searchRegistries(registries: string[], options?)

// Error types
export {
  RegistryError,
  RegistryNotFoundError,
  RegistryUnauthorizedError,
  RegistryForbiddenError,
  RegistryFetchError,
  RegistryNotConfiguredError,
  RegistryLocalFileError,
  RegistryParseError,
  RegistriesIndexParseError,
  RegistryMissingEnvironmentVariablesError,
  RegistryInvalidNamespaceError,
}
```

### 8. KEY REGISTRY UTILITIES

#### URL & Path Validation
```typescript
isUrl(path: string) => boolean
isLocalFile(path: string) => boolean      // Checks for .json extension
isLocalPath(path: string) => boolean      // Checks for ./ or /
isUniversalRegistryItem(item) => boolean  // Framework-agnostic check
```

#### Registry Item Parsing
```typescript
parseRegistryAndItemFromString(name: string) => {
  registry: string | null    // e.g., "@shadcn" or "@acme"
  item: string              // e.g., "button"
}
// Pattern: @namespace/itemname
```

#### File Deduplication
```typescript
deduplicateFilesByTarget(
  filesArrays: Array<RegistryItemFile[]>,
  config: Config
) => Promise<RegistryItemFile[]>
// Last file wins when same target path
```

#### Dependency Resolution
```typescript
recursivelyResolveFileImports(
  filePath: string,
  config: Config,
  projectInfo: ProjectInfo
) => Promise<{
  files: RegistryItemFile[]
  dependencies: string[]
}>
```

### 9. BUILTIN REGISTRIES & CONSTANTS

From `constants.ts`:
```typescript
REGISTRY_URL = process.env.REGISTRY_URL ?? "https://ui.shadcn.com/r"
FALLBACK_STYLE = "new-york-v4"  // Default when style not specified

BUILTIN_REGISTRIES = {
  "@shadcn": "${REGISTRY_URL}/styles/{style}/{name}.json"
}

BASE_COLORS = [
  "neutral", "zinc", "stone", "mauve", "olive", "mist", "taupe"
]

DEPRECATED_COMPONENTS = [
  { name: "toast", deprecatedBy: "sonner" },
  { name: "toaster", deprecatedBy: "sonner" }
]
```

### 10. KEY REGISTRY FUNCTIONS EXPLAINED

#### `resolveRegistryTree(names, config, options)`
**Purpose:** Resolve a list of items with ALL dependencies, returning a complete installation bundle.

**Process:**
1. Fetch requested items
2. Recursively fetch their `registryDependencies`
3. Handle namespace items (`@registry/item`)
4. Deduplicate files by target path
5. Deep merge: tailwind, cssVars, css, envVars, dependencies
6. Apply topological sort (Kahn's algorithm) to ensure dependencies come before dependents
7. Return consolidated object with files, deps, configs

**Return value:**
```typescript
{
  dependencies: string[]                // All npm packages needed
  devDependencies: string[]             // All dev packages
  files: RegistryItemFile[]             // All files to install
  tailwind: object                      // Merged tailwind config
  cssVars: object                       // Merged CSS variables
  css: object                           // Merged CSS snippets
  envVars: object                       // All env vars needed
  docs: string                          // Concatenated docs
  fonts?: RegistryFontItem[]            // Font definitions
}
```

#### `fetchRegistryItems(items, config, options)`
**Purpose:** Fetch raw registry items from various sources.

**Handles:**
- Local files (`.json`)
- HTTP URLs
- Namespaced items (`@registry/item`)
- Built-in items (`button`, `input`)

#### `searchRegistries(registries, options)`
**Purpose:** Fuzzy search across multiple registries.

**Features:**
- Fuzzy matching on name and description
- Pagination support
- Returns results with `addCommandArgument` (ready for CLI)

---

This comprehensive structure provides:
- **Flexibility**: Support for custom registries with auth
- **Modularity**: Clear separation of concerns
- **Composition**: Items depend on other items recursively
- **Framework-agnostic**: Universal items work anywhere
- **Type-safe**: Full Zod validation throughout
- **Extensible**: Easy to add new registry types and configurations

- **Built-in**: `button`, `input`, `dialog`
- **Namespaced**: `@acme/button`, `@custom/my-component`
- **URLs**: Full HTTP(S) URLs
- **Local files**: JSON files with `.json` extension

#### Dependency Resolution
The resolver (`resolver.ts`) handles:
1. **Direct dependencies**: Listed in `registryDependencies`
2. **Recursive resolution**: Fetches dependencies of dependencies
3. **Deduplication**: Ensures no duplicate items using topological sort
4. **File merging**: Combines files from all items in dependency tree
5. **Configuration merging**: Deep merges tailwind, cssVars, css configs

### 5. PACKAGE.JSON - BUILD & SCRIPTS

Key build information from `/packages/shadcn/package.json`:

**Build Tools:**
- `tsup` - TypeScript bundler
- `vitest` - Test runner
- `typescript` - TS compiler
- `ts-morph` - AST manipulation

**Key Dependencies:**
- `@babel/core`, `@babel/parser` - Code parsing
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` - MCP support
- `commander` - CLI framework
- `cosmiconfig` - Config file loader
- `deepmerge` - Deep object merging
- `fast-glob` - File globbing
- `zod` - Schema validation
- `tailwind-merge` - Tailwind utility merging
- `fuzzysort` - Fuzzy search
- `recast` - Code printing/formatting

**Build Scripts:**
```bash
npm run build              # Build with tsup
npm run dev               # Watch mode
npm run typecheck         # Type checking
npm start                 # Run CLI (uses dist/index.js)
npm test                  # Run vitest
npm run format:write      # Prettier formatting
npm run mcp:inspect       # Inspect MCP implementation
```

**Environment Variables for Local Development:**
```bash
REGISTRY_URL              # Registry base URL (default: https://ui.shadcn.com/r)
SHADCN_TEMPLATE_DIR       # Template directory path (for development)
```

### 6. CONFIGURATION FILES

#### components.json Schema
Located in `apps/v4/components.json` (example):
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json",
  "style": "new-york" | "new-york-v4",
  "rsc": true,                            // React Server Components
  "tsx": true,                            // Use TSX files
  "tailwind": {
    "config": "tailwind.config.ts",       // Path to tailwind config
    "css": "app/globals.css",             // Main CSS file
    "baseColor": "neutral",               // Theme color
    "cssVariables": true,                 // Use CSS variables
    "prefix": ""                          // Tailwind prefix
  },
  "aliases": {
    "components": "@/components",         // Component import alias
    "utils": "@/lib/utils",               // Utils alias
    "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui",    // UI components alias
    "lib": "@/lib",                       // Lib alias
    "hooks": "@/hooks"                    // Hooks alias
  },
  "iconLibrary": "lucide",                // Icon library
  "rtl": false,                           // Right-to-left support (optional)
  "menuColor": "default",                 // Menu styling
  "menuAccent": "subtle"                  // Menu accent style
}
```

#### Resolved Config Schema
After loading from components.json, config includes:
```typescript
{
  resolvedPaths: {
    cwd: string                           // Current working directory
    tailwindConfig: string                // Resolved tailwind config path
    tailwindCss: string                   # Resolved CSS file path
    utils: string                         // Resolved utils directory
    components: string                    // Resolved components directory
    lib: string                           // Resolved lib directory
    hooks: string                         // Resolved hooks directory
    ui: string                            // Resolved ui directory
  }
}
```

### 7. REGISTRY API EXPORTS

From `registry/index.ts`:
```typescript
// Main functions
export async function getRegistry(name: string, options?)
export async function getRegistryItems(items: string[], options?)
export async function resolveRegistryItems(items: string[], options?)
export async function getRegistries(options?)
export async function getRegistriesIndex(options?)  // Deprecated
export async function searchRegistries(registries: string[], options?)

// Error types
export {
  RegistryError,
  RegistryNotFoundError,
  RegistryUnauthorizedError,
  RegistryForbiddenError,
  RegistryFetchError,
  RegistryNotConfiguredError,
  RegistryLocalFileError,
  RegistryParseError,
  RegistriesIndexParseError,
  RegistryMissingEnvironmentVariablesError,
  RegistryInvalidNamespaceError,
}
```

### 8. KEY REGISTRY UTILITIES

#### URL & Path Validation
```typescript
isUrl(path: string) => boolean
isLocalFile(path: string) => boolean      // Checks for .json extension
isLocalPath(path: string) => boolean      // Checks for ./ or /
isUniversalRegistryItem(item) => boolean  // Framework-agnostic check
```

#### Registry Item Parsing
```typescript
parseRegistryAndItemFromString(name: string) => {
  registry: string | null    // e.g., "@shadcn" or "@acme"
  item: string              // e.g., "button"
}
// Pattern: @namespace/itemname
```

#### File Deduplication
```typescript
deduplicateFilesByTarget(
  filesArrays: Array<RegistryItemFile[]>,
  config: Config
) => Promise<RegistryItemFile[]>
// Last file wins when same target path
```

#### Dependency Resolution
```typescript
recursivelyResolveFileImports(
  filePath: string,
  config: Config,
  projectInfo: ProjectInfo
) => Promise<{
  files: RegistryItemFile[]
  dependencies: string[]
}>
```

### 9. BUILTIN REGISTRIES & CONSTANTS

From `constants.ts`:
```typescript
REGISTRY_URL = process.env.REGISTRY_URL ?? "https://ui.shadcn.com/r"
FALLBACK_STYLE = "new-york-v4"  // Default when style not specified

BUILTIN_REGISTRIES = {
  "@shadcn": "${REGISTRY_URL}/styles/{style}/{name}.json"
}

BASE_COLORS = [
  "neutral", "zinc", "stone", "mauve", "olive", "mist", "taupe"
]

DEPRECATED_COMPONENTS = [
  { name: "toast", deprecatedBy: "sonner" },
  { name: "toaster", deprecatedBy: "sonner" }
]
```

### 10. KEY REGISTRY FUNCTIONS EXPLAINED

#### `resolveRegistryTree(names, config, options)`
**Purpose:** Resolve a list of items with ALL dependencies, returning a complete installation bundle.

**Process:**
1. Fetch requested items
2. Recursively fetch their `registryDependencies`
3. Handle namespace items (`@registry/item`)
4. Deduplicate files by target path
5. Deep merge: tailwind, cssVars, css, envVars, dependencies
6. Apply topological sort (Kahn's algorithm) to ensure dependencies come before dependents
7. Return consolidated object with files, deps, configs

**Return value:**
```typescript
{
  dependencies: string[]                // All npm packages needed
  devDependencies: string[]             // All dev packages
  files: RegistryItemFile[]             // All files to install
  tailwind: object                      // Merged tailwind config
  cssVars: object                       // Merged CSS variables
  css: object                           // Merged CSS snippets
  envVars: object                       // All env vars needed
  docs: string                          // Concatenated docs
  fonts?: RegistryFontItem[]            // Font definitions
}
```

#### `fetchRegistryItems(items, config, options)`
**Purpose:** Fetch raw registry items from various sources.

**Handles:**
- Local files (`.json`)
- HTTP URLs
- Namespaced items (`@registry/item`)
- Built-in items (`button`, `input`)

#### `searchRegistries(registries, options)`
**Purpose:** Fuzzy search across multiple registries.

**Features:**
- Fuzzy matching on name and description
- Pagination support
- Returns results with `addCommandArgument` (ready for CLI)

---

This comprehensive structure provides:
- **Flexibility**: Support for custom registries with auth
- **Modularity**: Clear separation of concerns
- **Composition**: Items depend on other items recursively
- **Framework-agnostic**: Universal items work anywhere
- **Type-safe**: Full Zod validation throughout
- **Extensible**: Easy to add new registry types and configurations

1	import {
2	  iconLibraries,
3	  type IconLibrary,
4	  type IconLibraryName,
5	} from "shadcn/icons"
6	import { z } from "zod"
7	
8	import { BASE_COLORS, type BaseColor } from "@/registry/base-colors"
9	import { BASES, type Base } from "@/registry/bases"
10	import { bodyFonts, fonts, headingFonts } from "@/registry/fonts"
11	import { STYLES, type Style } from "@/registry/styles"
12	import { THEMES, type Theme } from "@/registry/themes"
13	
14	const SHADCN_VERSION = "latest"
15	
16	export { BASES, type Base }
17	export { STYLES, type Style }
18	export { THEMES, type Theme }
19	export { BASE_COLORS, type BaseColor }
20	export { bodyFonts, headingFonts, fonts }
21	export { iconLibraries, type IconLibrary, type IconLibraryName }
22	
23	export type BaseName = Base["name"]
24	export type StyleName = Style["name"]
25	export type ThemeName = Theme["name"]
26	export type BaseColorName = BaseColor["name"]
27	export type ChartColorName = Theme["name"]
28	
29	// Derive font values from registry fonts (e.g., "font-inter" -> "inter").
30	const fontValues = bodyFonts.map((f) => f.name.replace("font-", "")) as [
31	  string,
32	  ...string[],
33	]
34	const fontHeadingValues = ["inherit", ...fontValues] as const
35	
36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]
37	export type FontHeadingValue = (typeof fontHeadingValues)[number]
38	
39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {
52	  return `var(${getBodyFont(font)?.font.variable ?? "--font-sans"})`
53	}
54	
55	export const MENU_ACCENTS = [
56	  { value: "subtle", label: "Subtle" },
57	  { value: "bold", label: "Bold" },
58	] as const
59	
60	export type MenuAccent = (typeof MENU_ACCENTS)[number]
61	export type MenuAccentValue = MenuAccent["value"]
62	
63	export const MENU_COLORS = [
64	  { value: "default", label: "Default" },
65	  { value: "inverted", label: "Inverted" },
66	  { value: "default-translucent", label: "Default Translucent" },
67	  { value: "inverted-translucent", label: "Inverted Translucent" },
68	] as const
69	
70	export type MenuColor = (typeof MENU_COLORS)[number]
71	
72	export type MenuColorValue = MenuColor["value"]
73	
74	export const RADII = [
75	  { name: "default", label: "Default", value: "" },
76	  { name: "none", label: "None", value: "0" },
77	  { name: "small", label: "Small", value: "0.45rem" },
78	  { name: "medium", label: "Medium", value: "0.625rem" },
79	  { name: "large", label: "Large", value: "0.875rem" },
80	] as const
81	
82	export type Radius = (typeof RADII)[number]
83	
84	export type RadiusValue = Radius["name"]
85	
86	export const designSystemConfigSchema = z
87	  .object({
88	    base: z.enum(BASES.map((b) => b.name) as [BaseName, ...BaseName[]]),
89	    style: z.enum(STYLES.map((s) => s.name) as [StyleName, ...StyleName[]]),
90	    iconLibrary: z.enum(
91	      Object.keys(iconLibraries) as [IconLibraryName, ...IconLibraryName[]]
92	    ),
93	    baseColor: z
94	      .enum(
95	        BASE_COLORS.map((c) => c.name) as [BaseColorName, ...BaseColorName[]]
96	      )
97	      .default("neutral"),
98	    theme: z.enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ThemeName, ...ThemeName[]]),
99	    chartColor: z
100	      .enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ChartColorName, ...ChartColorName[]])
101	      .default("neutral"),
102	    font: z.enum(fontValues).default("inter"),
103	    fontHeading: z.enum(fontHeadingValues).default("inherit"),
104	    item: z.string().optional(),
105	    rtl: z.boolean().default(false),
106	    menuAccent: z
107	      .enum(
108	        MENU_ACCENTS.map((a) => a.value) as [
109	          MenuAccentValue,
110	          ...MenuAccentValue[],
111	        ]
112	      )
113	      .default("subtle"),
114	    menuColor: z
115	      .enum(
116	        MENU_COLORS.map((m) => m.value) as [MenuColorValue, ...MenuColorValue[]]
117	      )
118	      .default("default"),
119	    radius: z
120	      .enum(RADII.map((r) => r.name) as [RadiusValue, ...RadiusValue[]])
121	      .default("default"),
122	    template: z
123	      .enum([
124	        "next",
125	        "next-monorepo",
126	        "start",
127	        "react-router",
128	        "vite",
129	        "vite-monorepo",
130	        "react-router-monorepo",
131	        "start-monorepo",
132	        "astro",
133	        "astro-monorepo",
134	        "laravel",
135	      ])
136	      .default("next")
137	      .optional(),
138	  })
139	  .refine(
140	    (data) => {
141	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
142	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.theme)
143	    },
144	    (data) => ({
145	      message: `Theme "${data.theme}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
146	      path: ["theme"],
147	    })
148	  )
149	  .refine(
150	    (data) => {
151	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
152	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.chartColor)
153	    },
154	    (data) => ({
155	      message: `Chart color "${data.chartColor}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
156	      path: ["chartColor"],
157	    })
158	  )
159	
160	export type DesignSystemConfig = z.infer<typeof designSystemConfigSchema>
161

163	  base: "radix",
164	  style: "nova",

172	  rtl: false,
173	  menuAccent: "subtle",
174	  menuColor: "default",
175	  radius: "default",
176	  template: "next",
